patterns_recognized: 7 of 8 games today are neutralized to effective SKIP by hard caps already firing correctly (CAP1, CAP7, CAP10) or by natural D-grade dual-opposing signals — consistent with the archive's overall ~53.6% hit rate where caps preserve capital far more often than they cost it.

COL @ LAD (pick_prob=0.8671) crosses the HARD CAP 9 threshold (>0.80, validated 3-for-3 on 5/10-5/12 ATL@LAD/SF@LAD/SF@LAD and again 6/20 PIT@COL Skenes 82.2% loss) but grade_reasons shows only CAP 4 fired for this row (score 3->1) with no CAP 9 mention — the rule engine appears to have missed it. Applying CAP 9's intent manually as a DOWNGRADE.

TOR @ SF (SF) repeats the CAP 7 pick-side-bullpen-disadvantage pattern (0-for-7 backtest incl. 5/10 PIT@SF) on an otherwise elite pre-cap A/8 score. Archive shows overriding caps like this has been costly (TOR@ATL 6/4, BAL@LAD 6/21 both blew up after re-elevation) — confirming the cap despite the strong underlying stack.

Several SKIP rows carry bullpen_fatigue/acute_roster/confidence_downgrade stress flags (HOU@WSH, NYM@ATL, ARI@SD) — archive explicitly disconfirmed these as directional triggers (5/25: 3-of-4 stress-flag DOWNGRADEs missed real winners), so none were used to flip a pick today.
Postgame archive has a 15-day gap: last available file is 2026-06-21, none for 06-22 through 07-05, so cap-performance monitoring is running on data that is now roughly two weeks stale.

PHI @ KC.reasoning: HARD CAP 1 (edge -18.1pp on a PLATINUM pick) and HARD CAP 10 (pick_prob 0.507 in the bottom bucket, no CONFIRM) already collapse this to score 0. Archive validates CAP 1 4-for-5 live, and shows re-elevating a CAP-1-capped pick has blown up twice (TOR@ATL 6/4, BAL@LAD 6/21). Sánchez's real xERA edge is already priced past the model by the market (fair_prob 0.688 vs model 0.507) — an 18pp gap this large is the calibrator lagging an efficient market, not a market inefficiency. Confirming the double cap.

NYY @ TB.reasoning: Clean D-grade: TB's own Griffin Jax grades 2.23 xERA worse than Schlittler and PQI opposes TB (Δ=-26.2), while Stage 1/2 agree tightly (Δ=0.02). Archive explicitly tested treating this dual-opposing D-grade pattern as an anti-directional fade signal and found it uninformative (oscillated near coin-flip, e.g. 5/25 0-for-4 then 2-for-2) — so I'm not fading toward NYY, just confirming no edge exists on either side. No rookie SP or lineup anomaly changes that.
lessons_applied: D-grade dual-opposing-signal is uninformative, not anti-directional

HOU @ WSH.reasoning: Pre-cap this was a solid B+/3 (F-signal fires, PQI confirms Δ=-4.4, Stage 1/2 agree Δ=0.10), but HARD CAP 10 fires (pick_prob 0.506, bottom bucket, no CONFIRM boost) forcing score to 0. The bullpen_fatigue stress flag is present but archive shows stress flags are stake-size signals, not directional (5/25: 3-of-4 stress-flag DOWNGRADEs missed real winners) — doesn't change the call. Confirming the cap.

NYM @ ATL.reasoning: Peralta (NYM) grades 0.84 xERA better than López and PQI opposes ATL (Δ=-9.9) despite Stage 1/2 agreeing (Δ=0.07). Same clean dual-opposing D-grade pattern as NYY@TB — archive shows this combination is a coin-flip, not a fade trigger, so confirming SKIP rather than flipping to NYM. acute_roster/bullpen_fatigue flags present but treated as stake-size only per archive, not directional.

MIL @ STL.reasoning: No hard cap needed here — edge_pp is +4.26 (positive, CAP1 doesn't apply). SP edge (Dustin May) and PQI both oppose STL (Δ=-6.0) while Stage 1/2 agree tightly. Straightforward D-grade. Consistent with the archive finding that this signal combination is directionally uninformative rather than a fade signal, so I'm confirming SKIP rather than flipping to MIL.

ARI @ SD.reasoning: SP edge (Buehler, SD) and PQI both oppose ARI, with Stage 1/2 agreeing exactly (Δ=0.00). Naturally grades D/-2 with no hard cap needed. bullpen_fatigue and confidence_downgrade flags are present but archive shows these are non-directional stake-size signals only, not directional overrides. Confirming SKIP.

TOR @ SF.reasoning: Pre-cap this was an elite A/8 (Roupp's 0.34 xERA edge over Gausman, F-signal fires, Stage 1/2 agree Δ=0.01), but HARD CAP 7 fires on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0063 (SF's high-leverage pen is the worse one), then CAP 10 (pick_prob 0.564, bottom bucket, no CONFIRM) — score collapses 8→1→0. CAP 7's validating backtest is 0-for-7, including a prior SF loss (5/10 PIT@SF, 6-7). The gap is barely positive but CAP 7 is a validated bright-line rule, not magnitude-scaled — confirming both caps rather than re-litigating a strong pre-cap stack.
lessons_applied: CAP7 pick-side bullpen disadvantage, 0-for-7 incl. PIT@SF 5/10

COL @ LAD.reasoning: pick_prob=0.8671 clears the HARD CAP 9 threshold (>0.80), a bucket that historically hits only 33.3% actual vs 87.3% predicted — validated 3-for-3 on 5/10-5/12 (ATL@LAD, SF@LAD x2) and again 6/20 PIT@COL (Skenes 82.2%, lost). But grade_reasons only shows HARD CAP 4 firing on this row (Stage 1/2 Δ=0.22, confidence_downgrade=True, score 3→1); CAP 9 never applied despite clearly exceeding 0.80. This looks like a rule-engine miss. Downgrading to SKIP to apply CAP 9's intent, which the pipeline itself failed to enforce here.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP9 top-bucket hallucination applied manually (0.867>0.80; not triggered in grade_reasons)
